strategy: Expect a defensive counter for every damage buff you cast. Blades, traps (including Feint), auras, globals, and shadow polymorphs each trigger one of five defenses in order, so buff sparingly.
Lean on armor piercing and Shadow Shepherd for damage. Neither triggers his counter, so pierce is your safest way to build up without feeding his shields.
Attack with Fire (he takes +30% Fire); never Ice (81% resist) and expect 51% resist on everything else.
Before your kill hit, Shatter and Cleanse his stacked shields and damage-reduction, then punch through with armor piercing.
Drop him below full Health early and apply Doom and Gloom or Infection. His every-other-round interrupt becomes a heal once he is damaged, and you want to weaken it.
He is not stunable and has 30,930 Health, so bring a team with one dedicated hitter and let supports feed pierce and Shadow Shepherd.
cheatNotes: Every damage buff you cast (blade, trap, Feint, aura, global, or shadow polymorph) makes him cast the next of five defenses, then repeats after five. Buff sparingly and expect shields.
Shadow Shepherd is the exception, so cast it freely since it never triggers his counter.
Pure armor-piercing effects also do not trigger him, so pierce is your safest damage source.
The first counter is a 650-damage Spirit Armor on his whole side.
The second is Mass Tower Shield across his side.
The third is a -40% incoming damage aura for 4 Rounds.
The fourth is a -25% incoming damage global.
The fifth is Shadow Sentinel, then the cycle restarts.
While he is at full Health his every-other-Round interrupt is a 735 absorb shield, so more armor to clear.
Once he is below full Health that interrupt becomes a ~735 heal instead, so apply Doom and Gloom or Infection to weaken it.
